task_completion.description: The original grayscale portrait image is loaded into an image editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A grayscale portrait of a person is visible on the main canvas of an image editing application (like GIMP).

task_completion.description: A tool for converting the image to black and white (like Threshold) is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box for 'Threshold', 'Levels', 'Curves', or similar contrast/color manipulation tool is open over the image.

task_completion.description: The image is successfully converted to pure black and white.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image on the canvas no longer has smooth gray gradients; it consists of stark black and white pixels while still showing the recognizable features of the portrait.

task_completion.description: The agent initiates the process to save or export the modified image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export Image', 'Save As', or similar file dialog box is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The image is saved to the exact specified directory and filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file name field in the save/export dialog shows 'output.png' and the directory path indicates '/home/ga/'.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with the image editor's menus, dialogs, and canvas, rather than just a terminal window executing ImageMagick commands.

integrity.description: The agent manipulated the provided image rather than substituting it.
integrity.visual_evidence: The black and white image clearly matches the composition and subject of the original grayscale portrait.

integrity.description: The agent actually completed the save/export action.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the final 'Export' or 'Save' button in the dialog, or a subsequent screenshot shows the dialog closed after configuring the path.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original image is a 687x1031 grayscale portrait. The environment is GIMP. A successful conversion must eliminate mid-tone grays (less than 10% of pixels in the 31-224 intensity range) resulting in a pure binary black-and-white image (>=90% pure black or white). The result must preserve the subject's edges and not be a trivial solid color (>95% black or white is a failure). The final output must be saved exactly as '/home/ga/output.png'.
